name: Cystic Fibrosis Pedigree Chart with Inheritance Analysis
description: Create a 3-page ActivInspire flipchart for a high school biology genetics lesson on autosomal recessive inheritance. Page 1: Title the page 'Cystic Fibrosis Pedigree Analysis'. Below the title, create a legend showing that a square represents a male, a circle represents a female, and a filled (shaded) shape represents an affected individual. Page 2: Draw a three-generation pedigree chart. Generation I is an unaffected carrier couple: one male (square, labeled Aa) and one female (circle, labeled Aa), connected by a horizontal marriage line. Generation II consists of their four children arranged on a horizontal sibship line below, connected by vertical descent lines: an unaffected daughter (AA), a carrier son (Aa), an affected daughter (aa, filled), and a carrier son (Aa). The last carrier son marries a carrier female (Aa) from outside the family. Generation III consists of the three children of that couple: a carrier son (Aa), an affected son (aa, filled), and a carrier daughter (Aa). All affected individuals must be visually filled or shaded to distinguish them from unaffected ones. Page 3: Create a summary table with three columns headed Genotype, Phenotype, and Ratio, with rows for AA (Homozygous Dominant, Unaffected), Aa (Heterozygous, Carrier), and aa (Homozygous Recessive, Affected). Include the genotypic ratio 1:2:1 and phenotypic ratio 3:1 from the Generation I cross. Save the flipchart as cystic_fibrosis_pedigree.flipchart in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\Flipcharts\.
